scope: Built-in containers (Map/Set/Date), frozen-shallow leak, unbounded ledger memory growth

rounds.evidence: canonicalize({data: new Map([['k1',1],['k2',2]])}) === canonicalize({data: {}}) → TRUE. Map content totally lost — state with Map indistinguishable from empty.
rounds.fix: prepareForJson detects Map via instanceof, emits {[CANON_TAG_MAP]: sorted_entries}

rounds.evidence: canonicalize(new Set([1,2,3])) flattened to {} — Set content silently dropped
rounds.fix: prepareForJson detects Set via instanceof, emits {[CANON_TAG_SET]: sorted_items}

rounds.evidence: canonicalize({t: new Date(1000000)}) === canonicalize({t: new Date(1000001)}) → both '{}', two distinct timestamps indistinguishable
rounds.fix: prepareForJson detects Date via instanceof, emits {[CANON_TAG_DATE]: getTime()}

rounds.angle: Frozen sequence shallow — inner mutation possible
rounds.evidence: seq[0].turn = 999 succeeded after sequence() returned frozen array; fresh sequence() call returned mutated event (turn=999)
rounds.fix: stamp() now wraps each event in Object.freeze() before pushing to ledger — outer slice + inner objects both frozen

rounds.evidence: 50,000-turn encounter → 150,000 ledger events → +58 MB heap. Long-running PvP / raid encounters can OOM.
rounds.fix: createR67TickScheduler() now accepts config.max_ledger_size (default unbounded). When set, oldest events dropped (ring-buffer). server_tick continues monotonic increase.

rounds.evidence: When sched.guard(rt={auraGuard:null}) throws inside tickAuraGuard, sched.next_server_tick() = 0 and ledger length = 0 — server_tick NOT incremented mid-throw. Stamp is atomic.
Mutating caller's frame after appendFrame changes checksumStream hash — but this is EXPECTED reference semantics. Documented invariant: caller must not mutate frames after handing them off.
createCombatRuntime({encounterId: ''}) accepted by legacy. Stream with empty encounterId works (1 checkpoint emitted). Validation is OLD-code scope, not CMD1's adapter.

rounds.angle: Deterministic clock slot=0 boundary (turn=0 begin base=0)
rounds.evidence: step(0,'begin') = 0n; step(0,'aura_guard') = 1000n; monotonic preserved at boundary (last initialized to base_ns-1).
forensicDump on stream with 100K events in target turn — filter completes in 5ms. Acceptable. If needed, future enhancement: add events_limit option.

summary: Round 2 hunt found 5 more hidden bugs across container serialization (Map/Set/Date), encapsulation depth (frozen shallow), and memory bound (unbounded ledger). All 5 fixed with regression tests. 16 probes total — 5 bugs + 5 verified-no-bug + 6 acceptable behavior. Cumulative: 14 bugs fixed across both rounds, 39/39 tests pass, TSC exit 0, code-fix-clean rate 100%.
